paragraphs.background: I am a software developer, and I am incredibly proud of this. However, due to some unavoidable circumstances, I started my career quite late. I was born in 1997, which makes me 28 years old this year, and I only have a year and a half of experience in the industry.
paragraphs.experience: Before becoming a software engineer, I worked in a variety of roles, from tailor, glass door installer, and ironworker to installing electrical and plumbing systems for construction projects, running a personal business, and even making money online (MMO). These experiences have been invaluable. I once earned a significant amount of money in a short period of time, but I never truly found my passion and genuine interest in those jobs. Therefore, I decided to return to pursuing my unfinished dream. After many difficulties and challenges, I finally found my own goals, and now I am a software engineer.
paragraphs.passion: I genuinely love this job. I cherish the moments when I get to think and explore solutions to complex problems. I love the lively discussions with my teammates to find the best solutions to the challenges we face. I also love the times when I can smooth over disagreements between team members, because sometimes we can't agree on ideas. I find joy and excitement in every line of code, even if they are sometimes the result of "copy-pasting" from ChatGPT or Stack Overflow, haha.
paragraphs.strengths: Thanks to my unique experiences in the past, I feel that I have a solid and distinctive foundation compared to many other colleagues. I understand the value of discipline and self-reliance for personal development. I may be moving slower than some people, but I always persevere and move forward. To me, success means that today must be a little better than yesterday, my knowledge a little broader, and my experience a little richer. I have good communication skills with everyone, from the elderly to children, from women to men. I can recognize the strengths and weaknesses of others, and based on that, I can connect the members of the team together, creating a united and effective group.
paragraphs.learning: I have a special ability to observe and learn. I can observe how others work and reason to understand why they do it that way. From there, I can select their good points to learn from, while also learning from their mistakes.
paragraphs.closing: I am very much looking forward to the opportunity to work at your company. I am eager to develop myself and demonstrate my abilities through difficult challenges. I hope you will give me the opportunity to contribute to the development and success of your company.
paragraphs.thank: Thank you very much for taking the time to read this letter.
